name: add-backend-tool
description: Add a new tool to the backend OpenAI function calling system. Use when user mentions "new tool", "add tool", "backend function", "agent capability", or wants to extend what the AI agent can do.
capabilities: Read existing tool patterns in backend/main.py
Create new helper functions with proper error handling
Add tool definitions to the tools list
Implement tool invocation handlers in WebSocket message loop
Configure mode restrictions for chat versus agent mode
author: AppleLamps
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only instructions and guidance. No executable code, no file operations, no network calls, no command execution. All 23 static findings are false positives from pattern-matching on documentation code examples. The scanner misidentified YAML front matter, JSON metadata fields, markdown code blocks, and security documentation as security vulnerabilities.
valueStatement: Extending AI agent capabilities requires understanding backend tool patterns. This skill provides step-by-step instructions for adding new tools to the OpenAI function calling system, including helper functions, tool definitions, and security guardrails.
userTitle: Add Backend Tools for AI Agents
limitations: Does not modify files automatically - provides instructions only
Does not access external APIs directly
Requires manual implementation of tool logic
Does not validate security implications of new tools

useCases.title: Extend agent capabilities
useCases.description: Add new backend tools to enable AI agents to perform custom operations like file processing or API calls.

useCases.title: Integrate external services
useCases.description: Create tools that connect AI agents to external services, databases, or APIs for agent interactions.

useCases.title: Design tool frameworks
useCases.description: Establish patterns and security guardrails for tool development across teams or organizations.

promptTemplates.title: Basic tool addition
promptTemplates.scenario: Add a simple file listing tool
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a tool to list directory contents

promptTemplates.title: Git integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a tool for git operations

promptTemplates.title: Custom backend tool
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a tool that connects to my PostgreSQL database and runs read-only queries

bestPractices: Always include try-except error handling in helper functions
Add timeouts for long-running operations to prevent agent hanging
Document the tool purpose clearly in the function docstring
antiPatterns: Hardcoding API keys or secrets in tool implementations
Adding tools without reviewing security implications for Chat mode
Skipping error handling for external service calls

faq.question: Does this skill work with all Claude versions?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Tool patterns may vary slightly between versions.

faq.question: What are the limits on tool complexity?
faq.answer: Tools should be focused and single-purpose. Complex operations should be broken into multiple tools.

faq.question: How do I integrate with existing MCP tools?
faq.answer: Review backend/main.py for existing MCP integration patterns. Follow the same structure for consistency.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: This skill only provides instructions. It does not access files, execute code, or send data externally.

faq.question: Why is my new tool not working?
faq.answer: Check that you added both the helper function and the tool definition. Verify the handler in WebSocket loop.

faq.question: How is this different from MCP tools?
faq.answer: This skill adds backend tools to the function calling system. MCP tools are separate plugin-like integrations.
